channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: When is The Aniimo Closed Beta? Exact Start Time & End Date
item.content:encoded:
It’s been a great year for fans of cozy games, and while there’s still a while to go before a full release, the latest Aniimo closed beta is right around the corner.

Pawprint Studios’ creature-capture title has already garnered the attention of millions of fans worldwide, receiving over 15 million pre-registrations.

For all the details on the Aniimo closed beta, Insider Gaming has you covered.

When Does The Aniimo Closed Beta Start?

The Aniimo Closed Beta begins on Thursday, July 9, but no exact start time has been shared by Pawprint Studio yet. However, more details are imminent.

Sign-ups for the closed beta are available until July 5, and once that date passes, invitations for those selected to participate should start to be distributed.

  • Related: How to Sign Up For The Aniimo Closed Beta

That’s also when players can expect to discover the full details of exactly when the closed beta kicks off, but there may already be a hint of the start time.

Pawprint Studio has confirmed the Aniimo Closed Beta begins on July 9 in the CST time zone, eight hours ahead of UTC, so the doors could open on July 8 for those elsewhere in the world.

When Does The Aniimo Closed Beta End?

No end date has been shared by Pawprint Studio ahead of the start of the closed beta, with precise details expected to follow shortly.

The last closed beta began on January 23, 2026, and ended on February 8, 2026, so a similar duration could take place this time around. However, the end date may not be announced immediately.

The end of the last closed beta was announced on February 6, just two days before the servers closed, so there might not be much warning before the end.

However, all progress is wiped anyway, so you don’t have to worry too much.

Check out the Aniimo closed beta trailer below.

Are you excited for the release of Aniimo? Let us know in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For your next read, check out the free update in Mario Kart World, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1117)—Answers for Thursday, July 2
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch to that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (July 2)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • MOCKINGBIRD
  • FIELD MOUSE
  • TOM-TOM
  • COURT JESTER
  • MIME
  • LOOKING GLASS
  • TRACK RECORD
  • BILLY GOAT
  • DIAMOND RING
  • SPECTACLES
  • DAN DAN NOODLES
  • COPYCAT
  • TALKIE
  • RICH TEXT
  • T-1000
  • WATER CLOSET

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things that impersonate other things
  • Green – Older terms for things that we use today
  • Blue – Terms that start with shortened male names
  • Purple – Terms that start with words for sporting venues

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, July 2!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, July 2 (#1117)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – COPYCAT, MIME, MOCKINGBIRD, T-1000
  • Green – LOOKING GLASS, SPECTACLES, TALKIE, WATER CLOSET
  • Blue – BILLY GOAT, DAN DAN NOODLES, RICH TEXT, TOM-TOM
  • Purple – COURT JESTER, DIAMOND RING, FIELD MOUSE, TRACK RECORD

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-july-2-1117/

item.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Launching Earlier Than Planned
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has confirmed on social media that Onimusha: Way of the Sword will now release almost three weeks earlier than planned, dropping on September 4th instead of September 24th. In the post, the team didn’t explain why they’d made this change, but fans welcomed it nonetheless.

They explained that the pre-order bonus will now become an ‘early adopter’ bonus, and users will have until September 24th to claim it. Additionally, the demo will be updated to reflect the change, but it won’t get any new content.

Onimusha Makes a Rare Move

It’s not often that games are brought forward these days, but it does happen. Earlier this year, PRAGMATA, another Capcom title, was released a week earlier than planned.

It’s not all good news, though. Capcom admitted that things are a little bit awkward for Nintendo Switch 2 players:

As a result of the release date change, pre-orders for the Nintendo Switch 2 digital version will be automatically canceled. You will need to place a new pre-order on Nintendo eShop. We apologize for any inconvenience caused.

Pre-orders for the physical edition, including those placed via e-Capcom, will not be cancelled.

Perhaps the relocation is something to do with Grand Theft Auto 6 launching just three weeks after the original release date. But now, Onimusha: Way of the Sword will need to closely contend with The Blood of Dawnwalker, a somewhat similar game that launches just one day earlier.

It might also lose some players just ten days later when Marvel’s Wolverine hits the market.

  • RELATED: Onimusha Release Date Might Be Moved

Are you excited about the earlier release date for Onimusha: Way of the Sword? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Bethesda made a clever post about physical games, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Bethesda Makes Clever Oblivion ‘Physical’ Post As Sony Scraps Discs
item.content:encoded:
Earlier today, Sony revealed that from January 2028, all new PlayStation games will be digital only, with the tech titan scrapping the production of physical discs. It was an announcement that was met with staunch backlash on social media, prompting an outcry from developers and gamers alike.

Two hours after that announcement, the team at Bethesda Game Studios uploaded a short clip promoting the physical release of The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered on Switch 2. Thanks to the timing, it looked more like a tongue-in-cheek gut-punch to Sony, even if it wasn’t meant to be taken that way.

Oblivion Goes Physical on Switch 2

Two hours after Sony broke the news that PlayStation games are going all-digital, Bethesda’s social team posted a ten-second clip advertising the all-physical edition of Oblivion Remastered Deluxe Edition for Nintendo Switch 2. It’s dropping on August 11, bringing the masterful recreation to an all-new audience.

The timing of the post was most likely a mere coincidence, but it read like something sly from Bethesda:

Could the team have knocked up this snapshot meme in the two hours between Sony making the fateful announcement and Bethesda hitting publish? Perhaps.

Either way, fans took to the post with positivity (mostly) in the comment section. Many praised Bethesda for the move, talking up the benefits of physical media, while others lambasted the studio for not doing the same thing with prior ports, like Skyrim and Fallout 4.

On June 30, Bethesda posted a blog explaining how the Oblivion launch will work on Nintendo Switch 2, and it was in there that the physical edition was unveiled. That makes it even more likely that the clip above was merely in the bank and posted with some coincidental timing.

Are you going to buy Oblivion Remastered on Nintendo Switch 2? Let us know if you’re getting the physical edition on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Strands #850 Hints and Answers for Today, July 1, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #850 for Wednesday, July 1, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, July 1, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #850 are all related to evidence needed to solve mysteries.

General Strands Hints For July 1

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Fingerprints, DNA sampling, recorded conversations.
  • Hint 2: Early 2000s Disney Channel Original Movie with Lindsay Lohan: Get a ___.
  • Hint 3: Take a ___.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #850 for July 1, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for July 1, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • DIVE
  • GLUE
  • CANS
  • TELL
  • SIGN

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #850 on July 1, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • CLUE
  • INDICATION
  • INTIMATION
  • EVIDENCE
  • HINT
  • SPANAGRAM: TELLTALESIGN

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #850?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on July 2 will be Strands Game #851.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Eclipse Development Is on Track, Says Quantic Dream
item.content:encoded:
Quantic Dream has issued a new statement reassuring players that the development of Star Wars Eclipse is progressing as scheduled. This follows recent claims by striking staff members that they require additional resources and are understaffed.

Following the studio’s decision to discontinue the development of Spellcaster Chronicles, Insider Gaming reported that the revenue that the game would generate would help fund the development of the forthcoming Star Wars title. It was also previously reported that the development of Eclipse was going very slowly.

Then, one of the striking developers said that at least 115 individuals from the previous project could be brought on board to assist with this game’s release and that it wouldn’t be overstaffing at all. Rather, it would be giving the team what it needs. Now, the studio insists that, despite these claims, the development of Star Wars Eclipse is where it needs to be and that there are enough resources to cross the finish line.

Quantic Dream Says Star Wars Eclipse Has Enough Resources

Per a new statement given to IGN, Quantic Dream believes that the development team has everything it needs to succeed. They insist that the team has their full commitment and the necessary resources needed to complete “its mission.” Also, they state that their priority is to care for employees affected by the recent restructuring.

As previously stated, the development of Star Wars Eclipse remains unaffected by the recent closure of Spellcasters Chronicles, a decision driven by the challenging market conditions in that segment.

The development of Star Wars Eclipse is continuing as planned, with the full commitment of its team and all the necessary resources to complete its mission.

Our priority is to ensure that employees affected by the restructuring process receive the appropriate support, care, and consideration. Out of respect for this ongoing process and for the employees concerned, we will not provide any further comment at this stage.

  • More Star Wars Eclipse News: Star Wars Eclipse Plot Details Leak Online in “Controlled Leak”

In February 2025, reports claimed that the game’s development was unaffected by NetEase’s acquisition of Quantic Dream. Insider Gaming had previously reported that Quantic Dream was acquired for approximately 100 million euros.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. How do you feel about this news? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more on Insider Gaming, check out the recent cuts that have affected Bungie. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android
